insert_points.description: Express server imports planning decomposition entry point for orchestration.
Server wires task-plan execution into orchestration context.
Execution flow computes ETA after plan run.
Plan execution stage calls executeTaskPlan(plan, context).
Plan completion estimate derived via estimateCompletion(progress, plan).
planStepHandler wraps planning step inside session-aware queue handler.
Plan step invokes decomposeTask(effectivePrompt, clarifications).

env.name: PLANNING_URL
env.description: Base URL for remote planning microservice handling decomposition and plan execution.

risks: Long-running plan execution currently depends on in-process abort signals and trace context; service extraction must propagate cancellation tokens across network boundaries.
Plan execution streams progress telemetry via callbacks (logEvent, setProgress); external service requires SSE or webhook strategy to maintain parity.
Fixtures (plan.json, clarify.json, subtasks) persist to disk locally; remote service must return artifacts or provide signed URLs.
acceptance_criteria: Remote planning service reproduces TaskPlan validation and retry semantics (ClarificationRequiredError, SimplePromptBypassError).
execute-plan responses include progress snapshots compatible with existing PlanExecutionResult contract.
Monolith step queue can swap local calls with HTTP client configured by PLANNING_URL without changing orchestration APIs.

insert_points.description: Server imports multi-turn repair orchestrator.
Plan execution context delegates multiTurnRepair(context) for failed subtasks.
Single-run execution triggers multiTurnRepair after initial test failure.
Replay repair endpoint reuses multiTurnRepair for saved contexts.
Repair loop parses failure logs with analyzeFailure(initialLog).
LLM repair payload acquisition feeds strategy selection and diff generation.

env.name: REPAIR_URL
env.description: Base URL for remote repair microservice orchestrating analyze+repair flows.

risks: Repair payloads include large failure logs and diff artifacts; network service must support streaming or chunked uploads to avoid timeouts.
Current implementation accesses filesystem (gatherFileContext, applyArtifacts); remote service must expose artifact bundle contract or callback for file mutations.
RepairOnce fallback is mocked in tests but unused in production; ensure API parity even if monolith currently bypasses it.
acceptance_criteria: Multi-turn repair over HTTP preserves attempt ordering, telemetry hooks, and pause/abort semantics via sessionId.
Repair service validates payloads against RepairHistoryValidator before responding.
Monolith can toggle between local and remote repair via REPAIR_URL without altering step queue contract.

insert_points.description: Server sanitizes JSON model output before schema validation.
File persistence entry point used across plan + replay flows.
Single execution sanitizes parsed LLM output prior to validation.
Plan execution context delegates file writes to writeFiles(rootDir, files).
Single execution writes generated files before running tests.
Replay subtask endpoint replays stored files via writeFiles(projectRoot, files).
Subtask executor invokes context.writeFiles before test runs.

env.name: EXECUTOR_URL
env.description: Base URL for remote executor responsible for generation sanitization and file writes.

risks: File writes currently execute with full filesystem permissions; remote executor must expose artifact bundles without direct disk access.
Sanitization depends on schema versioning; service boundary requires explicit contract version negotiation.
Tests expect writeFiles to enforce path safety (no directory traversal); parity must be preserved over network responses.
acceptance_criteria: Remote executor returns payloads conforming to ExecutorOutput schema before validation in monolith.
File persistence operations respect existing allowlist (no writes outside project root).
Monolith can configure EXECUTOR_URL to swap local writeFiles/sanitization with HTTP calls without regressions.

insert_points.description: Server inspects prompt for missing context before question generation.
Clarification questions generated synchronously for /api/clarify and execute flow.
Prompt augmentation applied before execution when clarifications provided.
POST /api/clarify orchestrates detectMissing + generateQuestions pipeline.
Execution flow augments prompt with clarifications via augmentPrompt().
Fallback question generation when answers still leave gaps.

env.name: CLARIFICATION_URL
env.description: Base URL for clarification microservice managing question generation and prompt augmentation.

risks: Clarification logic caches questions per prompt (rememberClarificationQuestions); service extraction must persist cache or expose callback.
Synchronous clarification currently happens inline during /api/execute; remote call latency may impact UX and SSE timings.
Question templates rely on shared enums (MissingInfoType); require shared contract package to prevent drift.
acceptance_criteria: Clarification service returns deterministic question ordering for identical prompts.
Augmented prompts maintain existing formatting (Original request header).
Monolith can source clarification data from CLARIFICATION_URL during both /api/clarify and /api/execute flows.
